steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence sets up a contrast using 'but': robots designed to be comforting can backfire when they look too human, making people feel 'more unsettled than ______.' The blank names the positive emotional state the design was supposed to produce. Look for the word that matches the design intent of "comforting semblance of humanity."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — soothed: the passage says these robots are designed to create a "comforting" experience. Soothed is the positive, comforted state that the design aims for — but excessive human-likeness undermines it. ✓
  • Choice B — indifferent: feeling indifferent is neutral, not positive. A robot designed to comfort people wouldn't aim to make them indifferent. ✗
  • Choice C — revolted: revolted is even more negative than unsettled — you can't be more unsettled than revolted. The blank needs to be the positive baseline, not a worse reaction. ✗
  • Choice D — vindicated: vindicated means feeling justified or proven right — unrelated to how a comforting robot is supposed to make someone feel. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
